ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Singaporean economy rice with two vegetables and one meat. FOODON_00002694 (cooked rice food product) used as the closest defensible ancestor term.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (Rice dishes) is the closest available category. No specific code exists for the Singaporean economy rice combination format.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific mixed rice dish combination.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate of economy rice with two vegetable dishes and one meat dish, as typically served at a Singaporean hawker centre, weighing approximately 350 g.

nutrient_source.title: White Rice With 2 Veg & 1 Meat — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Composition
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 'White Rice With 2 Veg & 1 Meat' (cr_id G00010635). All per-100g values are sourced at confidence 0.95 as recorded in the input record.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; white rice (boiled) GI reference
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the composite Singaporean economy rice dish (white rice + 2 veg + 1 meat). GI of 72 is assigned based on the dominant carbohydrate component: boiled white rice (jasmine/long-grain type), GI ~72 per University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021). The vegetable and meat accompaniments contribute negligible carbohydrate and are not expected to materially alter the overall GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (72 × 88.2) / 100 ≈ 63.5, where carbohydrate per 350 g serving = 25.19 × 3.5 = 88.2 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 231 mg/100 g; partially offset by potassium 327 mg/100 g and dietary fibre 3.7 g/100 g
hypertension.guidance: Sodium at 231 mg/100 g is borderline for hypertension management; choose steamed or stir-fried vegetable preparations with minimal added salt and avoid high-sodium meat options such as braised pork belly, salted fish, or soy-sauce-heavy dishes to keep the full serving within a safe sodium range.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI white rice base (GI ~72) with 25.19 g carbohydrate per 100 g; glycemic load per standard serving approximately 63.5
type2_diabetes.guidance: The white rice base carries a GI of approximately 72 and delivers a high glycemic load per serving; individuals with type 2 diabetes should substitute white rice with brown rice or mixed-grain rice, eat vegetables and protein before rice to blunt postprandial glucose spikes, and limit portion size to no more than half a plate of rice.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate fat 4.21 g/100 g with unspecified saturated fat content; sodium 231 mg/100 g; dietary fibre 3.7 g/100 g is beneficial
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The two-vegetable component provides beneficial dietary fibre (3.7 g/100 g) and potassium (327 mg/100 g) that support cardiovascular health, but the unspecified meat component may contribute saturated fat; choose lean meats such as steamed fish or tofu and avoid deep-fried or braised fatty meat preparations to minimise cardiovascular risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Unspecified meat component may be red meat (pro-inflammatory arachidonic acid); white rice is a refined carbohydrate that may promote low-grade inflammation
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Choose fatty fish or tofu as the protein component and select cruciferous vegetables (broccoli, bok choy, cabbage) to maximise anti-inflammatory benefit; avoid red meat preparations as arachidonic acid amplifies inflammatory pathways relevant to rheumatoid arthritis.

llm_training.natural_language_description: White Rice with 2 Vegetables and 1 Meat is a quintessential Singaporean hawker-centre economy rice (cai fan) combination. It consists of a base of steamed white jasmine rice served with two self-selected vegetable side dishes and one meat or protein dish, all ladled from a display counter. The vegetables are typically stir-fried leafy greens (kangkong, bok choy, cabbage, long beans) or braised tofu, while the meat component may be steamed fish, braised pork, chicken curry, or other hawker preparations. The dish is moderately energy-dense at 164 kcal per 100 g, with a balanced macronutrient profile of 6.5 g protein, 4.21 g fat, and 25.19 g carbohydrate per 100 g. Dietary fibre is notably high at 3.7 g/100 g due to the two vegetable portions, and potassium at 327 mg/100 g supports blood pressure management. Sodium at 231 mg/100 g is borderline and depends heavily on the cooking style of the chosen dishes. The white rice base carries a high glycemic index (~72), making portion control important for individuals managing blood glucose. This combination is one of the most commonly consumed lunch and dinner meals in Singapore across all ethnic groups and is widely available at hawker centres, coffee shops, and food courts. The dish is culturally associated with Malay and Chinese hawker traditions and is known colloquially as 'cai fan' in Hokkien-influenced Singaporean Mandarin.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010635) at confidence 0.95, which is the primary approved source for Singaporean dishes. The record is marked as canonical and reviewed. GI is estimated from the dominant white rice component using the University of Sydney GI Database, which is the approved GI source.
notes: saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite hawker dish is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for G00010635. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data beyond the reported minerals are not available in the HPB Foodo source for this composite dish as of 2026. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 as reported by HPB Foodo; this is plausible given the unspecified meat component may be lean or plant-based in the averaged dataset. GI of 72 is an analogous estimate based on boiled white rice (jasmine type) from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement exists for this composite dish. Glycemic load per serving calculated at 350 g serving size: GL = (72 × 88.17) / 100 ≈ 63.5. The halal flag is set to true reflecting the Malay cultural context of this dish as recorded in the input; individual preparations may vary. Dietary flags reflect the most common preparation; actual allergen and dietary status depend on the specific meat and vegetable choices made at point of service.
